8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":

1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

2	from typing import List
3	
4	from .models import Bookmark
5	
6	
7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463687240-6t84vh/src/cli.py`**

The CLI uses `argparse` to build a command-line interface with subcommands. The `build_parser()` function defines:

- **add** - Add a new bookmark
  - Arguments: `url` (positional), `title` (positional)
  - Options: `--tags` (multiple), `--notes`

- **list** - List all bookmarks
  - Options: `--format` (choices: "table" or "json", default: "table")

- **delete** - Delete a bookmark
  - Arguments: `id` (positional, bookmark ID)

- **show** - Show a single bookmark
  - Arguments: `id` (positional, bookmark ID)

The `main()` function handles command dispatching and calls appropriate storage/formatting functions.

### 3. Data Model for Bookmarks

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463687240-6t84vh/src/models.py`**

```python
@dataclass
class Bookmark:
    id: str
    url: str
    title: str
    tags: List[str] = []  # default empty list
    notes: str = ""  # default empty string
    created_at: str  # ISO format datetime
```

Fields:
- **id**: UUID string (generated server-side)
- **url**: Full URL string
- **title**: Bookmark title (user-provided)
- **tags**: List of string tags for categorization
- **notes**: Optional notes/description
- **created_at**: ISO format datetime string (auto-generated at creation)

The Bookmark class includes:
- `to_dict()`: Serializes to dictionary for JSON storage
- `from_dict(cls, data)`: Factory method to deserialize from dictionary

### 4. Storage Layer

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463687240-6t84vh/src/storage.py`**

Storage Format: JSON file at `~/.bookmarks.json`

Functions:
- **load_bookmarks(db_path)**: Loads all bookmarks from JSON, returns empty list if file doesn't exist
- **save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)**: Persists list of bookmarks to JSON with indentation (2 spaces)
- **add_bookmark(url, title, tags, notes, db_path)**: Creates new bookmark with UUID, adds to list, saves
- **delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)**: Filters out bookmark, saves, returns bool success
- **get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)**: Returns single bookmark or None

All functions accept an optional `db_path` parameter (default: `~/.bookmarks.json`) for testing flexibility.

### 5. Output Formatting

**File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463687240-6t84vh/src/formatter.py`**

Two output formats:

- **format_table(bookmarks)**: Plain-text table format
  - Columns: ID (8 chars), Title (30 chars), URL (40 chars), Tags (20 chars)
  - Column values are truncated to fit widths
  - ID is shortened to first 8 characters
  - Tags are comma-separated
  - Returns "No bookmarks found." for empty lists

- **format_json(bookmarks)**: JSON format
  - Uses `json.dumps()` with 2-space indentation
  - Outputs list of bookmark dictionaries with all fields

### 6. Test Structure

**Files:**

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463687240-6t84vh/tests/test_search.py`

**Testing Patterns:**
- Uses pytest framework
- Fixtures: `db_path` (temporary file via pytest's `tmp_path`), `sample_bookmarks` (test data), `bookmarks` (search test data)
- Organized as test classes (e.g., `TestLoadBookmarks`, `TestAddBookmark`)
- Test methods follow `test_` naming convention
- Uses assertions for validation

**Existing Tests (test_storage.py):**
- TestLoadBookmarks: Load from file, empty file handling
- TestSaveBookmarks: File creation, JSON validity
- TestAddBookmark: Bookmark creation, persistence, UUID generation
- TestDeleteBookmark: Deletion, missing ID handling
- TestGetBookmark: Retrieval by ID, missing ID handling

**Planned Tests (test_search.py):**
- The file exists and defines expected tests for a `search` module that DOESN'T exist yet
- Tests import from `src.search` (not yet created)
- Expected functions: `search()`, `search_by_title()`, `search_by_tag()`, `search_by_domain()`

### 7. Existing Utilities for Filtering/Querying Bookmarks

**Current Status:** NONE - No filtering/searching utilities currently exist in the codebase.

The test file `test_search.py` defines the expected API for search functionality that needs to be implemented. The tests are the specification:

**Expected search_by_title(bookmarks, query) function:**
- Case-insensitive substring matching in title field
- Returns matching bookmarks

**Expected search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag) function:**
- Exact tag match (not substring)
- Returns bookmarks containing the tag
- Example: `tag="docs"` finds bookmarks with "docs" in tags list

**Expected search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain) function:**
- Domain/subdomain matching from URL field
- Handles both exact domain (github.com) and subdomains (docs.python.org)
- Returns matching bookmarks

**Expected search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None) function:**
- Combined search with AND logic (all criteria must match)
- Supports zero, one, or multiple criteria
- No criteria returns all bookmarks
- Conflicting criteria (Flask + JavaScript tag) returns empty list

**Expected CLI integration:**
- `search` command in CLI parser
- Flags: `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain`, `--format` (table/json)

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add `search` Command to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI manages a local JSON-based bookmark store but provides no way to filter or find bookmarks. Users need to search by title substring, exact tag, and/or domain, with AND logic when multiple criteria are combined. The test file `tests/test_search.py` already exists and fully specifies the expected API — all tests currently fail because `src/search.py` doesn't exist and the CLI has no `search` subcommand.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| **Create** | `src/search.py` |
| **Modify** | `src/cli.py` |

---

## Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

Create a new module with four public functions. The tests import them as:
```python
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title
```

### `search_by_title(bookmarks, query) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Case-insensitive substring match on `b.title`
- Implementation: `[b for b in bookmarks if query.lower() in b.title.lower()]`

### `search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Exact match: `tag` must appear in `b.tags` list (not a substring — the test uses `"flask"` and expects only the Flask bookmark)
- Implementation: `[b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]`

### `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Extract `netloc` from each bookmark's URL using `urllib.parse.urlparse`
- Match if `netloc == domain` (exact) **or** `netloc.endswith("." + domain)` (subdomain)
- This handles the two test cases:
  - `"github.com"` → exact-matches `github.com` netloc (2 results)
  - `"docs.python.org"` → exact-matches `docs.python.org` netloc (2 results)
- Implementation:
  ```python
  from urllib.parse import urlparse
  def search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain):
      def matches(b):
          netloc = urlparse(b.url).netloc
          return netloc == domain or netloc.endswith("." + domain)
      return [b for b in bookmarks if matches(b)]
  ```

### `search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Combined filter with AND logic: applies each non-None criterion sequentially
- No criteria → returns all bookmarks (pass-through)
- Implementation:
  ```python
  def search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None):
      result = bookmarks
      if title is not None:
          result = search_by_title(result, title)
      if tag is not None:
          result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
      if domain is not None:
          result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
      return result
  ```

---

## Step 2 — Update `src/cli.py`

### 2a — Add import
At the top with other imports, add:
```python
from .search import search
```

### 2b — Add `search` subparser in `build_parser()`
After the existing `show` subparser block (before `return parser`):
```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag",   default=None, help="Filter by exact tag")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

The CLI tests verify:
- `parser.parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])` → `args.command == "search"`, `args.title == "python"`
- `parser.parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])` → `args.format == "json"`

### 2c — Add `search` handler in `main()`
After the `elif args.command == "show":` block:
```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

No new imports needed for `load_bookmarks`, `format_table`, or `format_json` — they are already imported in `cli.py`.

---

## Verification

Run the full test suite:
```bash
pytest tests/ -v
```

Expected: all tests in `tests/test_storage.py` (previously passing) and `tests/test_search.py` (currently failing) pass.

Manual smoke test:
```bash
bookmark add https://github.com/pallets/flask "Flask" --tags python flask web
bookmark add https://docs.python.org/3/ "Python Docs" --tags python docs
bookmark search --title python
bookmark search --tag flask
bookmark search --domain github.com
bookmark search --title python --tag docs --format json
```

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(

    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

            tags=args.tags,

            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))
